content.text:
You are the Researcher subagent.

Your job is to gather external context, compare approaches, and summarize source-backed findings for the main agent.

Use the native-web-search skill when internet research is needed.

If browser or web automation tools are available, you may use them for verification, but do not modify local repo files.

Todo coordination:
- If the todo tool is available and the task maps to an existing todo, use it actively.
- Start by listing or reading relevant todos when that helps orient the task.
- Keep todo body notes current with concrete findings, progress, blockers, and validation evidence.
- If you finish a todo-shaped task, update/close the todo when appropriate instead of leaving stale state.
- If another session owns a todo, do not steal it casually; note the conflict in your final output.

Your final response must use the exact required section headings for your role. Keep it concise but complete. Include concrete file paths, commands, URLs, and evidence where relevant.

Required final sections:

## Question

## Sources

## Findings

## Implications

## Open Questions

The Harness todo extension is preloaded in this subagent. Use the todo tool directly when it helps coordinate or validate work.

Native web search helper:
- Use bash to run node /Users/roshan/dev/harness/skills/native-web-search/search.mjs "query" for fast web research when external context is needed.
- Summarize the returned sources and findings with URLs in your final answer.

Browser verification helper:
- Use bash to run /Users/roshan/dev/harness/skills/web-browser/scripts/cdp.mjs list to discover tabs.
- Then use commands like /Users/roshan/dev/harness/skills/web-browser/scripts/cdp.mjs snap <target>, html <target>, click <target> "selector", type <target> "text", or shot <target> to verify UI flows.
- Prefer real browser evidence over guessing for UI assertions.

Top-level delegated task:

Do a quick internet research test run about current best practices for role-based coding subagents and native web search in AI coding agents. Return 5-8 concise bullets with explicit full source URLs and note any disagreements between sources.

Keep the work scoped to your role. If the todo tool is available, use it deliberately instead of merely mentioning it. If browser helpers are available and the task implies UI verification, use them when appropriate.

content.text:
---
name: native-web-search
description: "Trigger native web search. Use when you need quick internet research with concise summaries and full source URLs."
---

# Native Web Search

Use this skill to run a **fast model with native web search enabled** and get a concise research summary with explicit full URLs.

## Script

- `search.mjs`

## Usage

Run from this skill directory:

```bash
node search.mjs "<what to search>" --purpose "<why you need this>"
```

Examples:

```bash
node search.mjs "latest python release" --purpose "update dependency notes"
node search.mjs "vite 7 breaking changes" --purpose "prepare migration checklist"
```

Optional flags:

- `--provider openai-codex|anthropic`
- `--model <model-id>`
- `--timeout <ms>`
- `--json`

## Output expectations

The script instructs the model to:
- search the internet for the requested topic
- provide a concise summary for the given purpose
- include full canonical URLs (`https://...`) for each key finding
- highlight disagreements between sources

## Notes

- No extra npm install is required.
- If module resolution fails, set `PI_AI_MODULE_PATH` to `@mariozechner/pi-ai`'s `dist/index.js` path.
- If OAuth helper resolution fails, set `PI_AI_OAUTH_MODULE_PATH` to `@mariozechner/pi-ai`'s `dist/oauth.js` path.
- For OAuth providers, the script can fall back to a still-valid cached `access` token from `~/.pi/agent/auth.json`.

---
name: web-browser
description: Interact with a live local Chrome-family browser session over CDP, using target-based commands for listing tabs, inspecting pages, clicking, typing, and debugging.
license: Stolen from Mario
---

# Web Browser Skill

Target-based Chrome DevTools Protocol tooling for live browser sessions.

> Harness note: this skill now centers on `scripts/cdp.mjs`. Helper scripts for cookie dismissal, picking, and logging remain available, but the primary workflow is tab selection via `list` and a target prefix.

## Prerequisites

- Chrome-family browser with remote debugging enabled in `chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging`
- Node.js 22+
- Optional: set `CDP_PORT_FILE` if your browser stores `DevToolsActivePort` in a non-standard location

Supported browser discovery includes Chrome, Chromium, Brave, Edge, and Vivaldi.

## Primary workflow

List open tabs first:

```bash
./scripts/cdp.mjs list
```

Use the unique target prefix from `list` for all tab commands.

## Core commands

```bash
./scripts/cdp.mjs list
./scripts/cdp.mjs shot <target> [file]
./scripts/cdp.mjs snap <target>
./scripts/cdp.mjs html <target> [".selector"]
./scripts/cdp.mjs eval <target> "expression"
./scripts/cdp.mjs nav <target> https://example.com
./scripts/cdp.mjs net <target>
./scripts/cdp.mjs click <target> "selector"
./scripts/cdp.mjs clickxy <target> <x> <y>
./scripts/cdp.mjs type <target> "text"
./scripts/cdp.mjs loadall <target> "selector"
./scripts/cdp.mjs evalraw <target> <method> [json]
./scripts/cdp.mjs open [url]
./scripts/cdp.mjs stop [target]
```

Notes:
- `clickxy` uses CSS pixels, not screenshot image pixels
- `type` uses CDP input events and is better than JS eval for text entry
- first access to a tab may trigger Chrome's approval prompt; repeated commands reuse a persistent daemon

## Helper scripts

### Pick elements

```bash
./scripts/pick.js "Click the submit button"
```

Interactive picker for the active page.

### Dismiss cookie dialogs

```bash
./scripts/dismiss-cookies.js
./scripts/dismiss-cookies.js --reject
```

Runs against the active page and attempts to accept or reject common consent dialogs.

### Background logging

```bash
./scripts/watch.js
./scripts/logs-tail.js
./scripts/logs-tail.js --follow
./scripts/net-summary.js
```

Writes JSONL logs to:

```text
~/.cache/agent-web/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/<targetId>.jsonl
```

## Legacy wrappers

These wrappers now delegate to `cdp.mjs`:

```bash
./scripts/nav.js <target> <url>
./scripts/eval.js <target> "expression"
./scripts/screenshot.js <target> [file]
```

## Best practices

- Prefer stable selectors over index-based DOM access across multiple commands
- Use `snap` for semantic structure and `html` when you need exact markup
- Use `evalraw` for unsupported CDP methods instead of adding one-off scripts
- Use browser interaction only when the user explicitly wants page inspection or manipulation

content.text:
## Question

Quick internet-research test run on current best practices for:
1. role-based coding subagents, and
2. native web search in AI coding agents.

Note: I checked local Harness skill docs at `skills/native-web-search/SKILL.md` and `skills/web-browser/SKILL.md`, but this session did **not** expose a shell/browser tool, so I could not run the intended commands:
- `node skills/native-web-search/search.mjs "<query>" --purpose "..."`
- `skills/web-browser/scripts/cdp.mjs list`

The findings below are a best-effort synthesis from stable public docs/announcements known to me, **not freshly fetched in-session**.

## Findings

- **Best practice: keep subagents role-scoped and capability-scoped.** Vendor guidance on agent/tool design consistently favors narrow specialists over one giant agent: e.g. “researcher”, “coder”, “reviewer”, each with bounded tools and explicit handoff contracts. Sources: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents , https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents

- **Use structured delegation artifacts, not freeform chat, between subagents.** The common pattern is passing task briefs, constraints, and expected output schema so the main agent can merge results predictably. MCP and A2A both push explicit interfaces/protocols over ad hoc prompt passing. Sources: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/ , https://google.github.io/A2A/

- **Give researcher-style subagents read-only access and citation duties.** For external-context gathering, current best practice is to separate “search/summarize/cite” from “edit/execute” to reduce accidental changes and improve auditability. Sources: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-web-search , https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/web-search

- **Native web search should return source URLs and uncertainty/disagreement notes.** OpenAI/Anthropic/Google grounding/search docs all emphasize grounded answers with citations; for coding agents, that translates to full URLs, version-aware notes, and explicit mention when sources conflict. Sources: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-web-search , https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/web-search , https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/grounding

- **Prefer tool-mediated search over “pretend browsing.”** Current platform docs treat search/browse as explicit tools with controllable invocation, not as an unstated model behavior. That improves traceability, safety, and reproducibility in coding workflows. Sources: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents , https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/tool-use/overview

- **For coding agents, combine search with retrieval/protocol layers rather than making web search the only context source.** MCP/LangChain-style patterns suggest using local repo state, docs retrieval, and web search together: local truth for code, web for freshness. Sources: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/ , https://docs.langchain.com/

- **Disagreement:** some sources favor lightweight single-agent orchestration first, while others formalize multi-agent protocols sooner. Anthropic’s “building effective agents” messaging generally warns against unnecessary complexity, while MCP/A2A ecosystems lean harder into standardized multi-agent/tool interoperability. Sources: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents , https://google.github.io/A2A/ , https://modelcontextprotocol.io/

## Implications

- For Harness-style coding workflows, a good default is:
  - main agent = planner/editor,
  - researcher subagent = web search + citations only,
  - reviewer subagent = validation/checks only.
- Handoffs should be structured: question, scope, sources, findings, open questions.
- Native web search should be invoked explicitly and its outputs preserved with full canonical URLs.
- When tools are available, the intended local command path here is:
  - `node skills/native-web-search/search.mjs "<query>" --purpose "<why>"`
- If you want a stronger result, rerun this task in a session with shell access so I can execute the search script and return fresh, verified citations.
